text: Okay, this is very simple, this reactor. Step 1, still this is the cathode flow plate.
It also has a spinning flow field.
Step 2, this is a cathode electrode.
The catalyst is still prepared by spray-coated oxide catalysis onto L2-3-6-3 carbon paper.
Step 3.
Step 3, this is a cathode gasket.
The gas thickness is 0.3 µm, and the INR window size is 1x1 cm.
Step 4, we place the cathode side membrane.
The cathode side membrane is still X3-750.
Step 5, this is the middle electrolyte chamber.
The chamber thickness is 3 µm, but the window size is increased to 3x3 cm.
And a flow channel is also added inside the middle chamber.
This chamber is used to hold and circulate the liquid electrolyte.
The electrolyte is one molecule each, and it will be continuously circulated through the middle chamber during operation.
Compared to the previous design, the key change in this reactor is that the middle chamber thickness remains 3 µm,
while the chamber window is enlarged to 3 cm by 3 cm.
This increases the electrolyte volume in the middle chamber and improves electrolyte distribution through the added flow channel.
Step 6, we will add the anode side membrane.
Step 7, we will add the anode side membrane.
Step 8, we will add a bigger membrane in another side.
Then we place the anode gasket.
The anode side gasket is still 0.3 µm.
Step 8 is the anode electrode.
The anode is prepared by spray-coating.
The anode electrode is pre-spray-coated with PT ruthenium carbon catalysis on the L2-3-C3 carbon paper.
The catalyst loading is 2 µg%².
Step 9, this is the anode flow plate. It also has a spinting flow field.
Finally, after aligning all components, we will assemble and tighten the reactor using a toggle 2 Nm.
Step 10, this is the anode side membrane.
This reactor is tightened using 4 bolts in a diagonal sequence.
This reactor is tightened using 4 bolts in a diagonal sequence.
This reactor is tightened using 4 bolts in a diagonal sequence.
This reactor is tightened using 4 bolts in a diagonal sequence.
In summary, this reactor keeps the 3 µm splitting one molecule each middle chamber, but increases the chamber window to 3 by 3 cm and adds an internal flow channel.
This design is intended to increase the electrolyte volume, improve electrolyte distribution, and maintain a shorter ion transport distance.
